events.content:
Build a command palette using Nuxt UI's `CommandPalette` component with grouped commands.

Requirements:

1. Use the `UCommandPalette` component with the `groups` prop
2. Create at least 2 groups:
   - **Actions** group (`id: 'actions'`) with items like "New File", "New Folder", "Save"
   - **Navigation** group (`id: 'navigation'`) with items like "Home", "Settings", "Profile"
3. Each item must have a `label` and an `icon` prop (use Lucide icons like `i-lucide-file-plus`)
4. At least one item should have `kbds` (keyboard shortcut hints), e.g. `['meta', 'N']`
5. At least one item should have an `onSelect` callback that logs to console
6. Bind the selected value with `v-model`

The palette should be visible on the page (not hidden behind a keyboard shortcut).

message.content:
Build a command palette using Nuxt UI's `CommandPalette` component with grouped commands.

Requirements:

1. Use the `UCommandPalette` component with the `groups` prop
2. Create at least 2 groups:
   - **Actions** group (`id: 'actions'`) with items like "New File", "New Folder", "Save"
   - **Navigation** group (`id: 'navigation'`) with items like "Home", "Settings", "Profile"
3. Each item must have a `label` and an `icon` prop (use Lucide icons like `i-lucide-file-plus`)
4. At least one item should have `kbds` (keyboard shortcut hints), e.g. `['meta', 'N']`
5. At least one item should have an `onSelect` callback that logs to console
6. Bind the selected value with `v-model`

The palette should be visible on the page (not hidden behind a keyboard shortcut).

3012:\> This change affects the \`Toast\` component as well as all component that have \`items\` links like \`NavigationMenu\`, \`DropdownMenu\`, \`CommandPalette\`, etc.
3420:- The `CommandPalette` component `groups` prop structure has changed. Each group now has an `items` array and uses `onSelect` instead of `click`:
17572:\> It's used this way in the \[\`CommandPalette\`]\(/docs/components/command-palette), \[\`InputMenu\`]\(/docs/components/input-menu), \[\`Select\`]\(/docs/components/select) or \[\`SelectMenu\`]\(/docs/components/select-menu) components for example.

tool.name: unknown
tool.result:
18796	# CommandPalette
18797	
18798	## Usage
18799	
18800	Use the `v-model` directive to control the value of the CommandPalette or the `default-value` prop to set the initial value when you do not need to control its state.
18801	
18802	```vue
18803	<script setup lang="ts">
18804	import type { CommandPaletteGroup } from '@nuxt/ui'
18805	</script>
18806	
18807	<template>
18808	  <UCommandPalette class="flex-1 h-80" />
18809	</template>
18810	```
18811	
18812	\> \[!TIP]
18813	\> See: #control-selected-items
18814	\> You can also use the \`@update\:model-value\` event to listen to the selected item(s).
18815	
18816	### Groups
18817	
18818	The CommandPalette component filters groups and ranks matching commands by relevance as users type. It provides dynamic, instant search results for efficient command discovery. Use the `groups` prop as an array of objects with the following properties:
18819	
18820	- `id: string`{.shiki,shiki-themes,material-theme-lighter,material-theme,material-theme-palenight lang="ts-type"}
18821	- `label?: string`{.shiki,shiki-themes,material-theme-lighter,material-theme,material-theme-palenight lang="ts-type"}
18822	- `slot?: string`{.shiki,shiki-themes,material-theme-lighter,material-theme,material-theme-palenight lang="ts-type"}
18823	- `items?: CommandPaletteItem[]`{.shiki,shiki-themes,material-theme-lighter,material-theme,material-theme-palenight lang="ts-type"}
18824	- [`ignoreFilter?: boolean`{.shiki,shiki-themes,material-theme-lighter,material-theme,material-theme-palenight lang="ts-type"}](https://ui.nuxt.com/#with-ignore-filter)
18825	- [`postFilter?: (searchTerm: string, items: T[]) => T[]`{.shiki,shiki-themes,material-theme-lighter,material-theme,material-theme-palenight lang="ts-type"}](https://ui.nuxt.com/#with-post-filtered-items)
18826	- `highlightedIcon?: string`{.shiki,shiki-themes,material-theme-lighter,material-theme,material-theme-palenight lang="ts-type"}
18827	
18828	\> \[!CAUTION]
18829	\> You must provide an \`id\` for each group otherwise the group will be ignored.
18830	
18831	Each group contains an `items` array of objects that define the commands. Each item can have the following properties:
18832	
18833	- `prefix?: string`{.shiki,shiki-themes,material-theme-lighter,material-theme,material-theme-palenight lang="ts-type"}
18834	- `label?: string`{.shiki,shiki-themes,material-theme-lighter,material-theme,material-theme-palenight lang="ts-type"}
18835	- `suffix?: string`{.shiki,shiki-themes,material-theme-lighter,material-theme,material-theme-palenight lang="ts-type"}
18836	- `icon?: string`{.shiki,shiki-themes,material-theme-lighter,material-theme,material-theme-palenight lang="ts-type"}
18837	- `avatar?: AvatarProps`{.shiki,shiki-themes,material-theme-lighter,material-theme,material-theme-palenight lang="ts-type"}
18838	- `chip?: ChipProps`{.shiki,shiki-themes,material-theme-lighter,material-theme,material-theme-palenight lang="ts-type"}
18839	- `kbds?: string[] | KbdProps[]`{.shiki,shiki-themes,material-theme-lighter,material-theme,material-theme-palenight lang="ts-type"}
18840	- `active?: boolean`{.shiki,shiki-themes,material-theme-lighter,material-theme,material-theme-palenight lang="ts-type"}
18841	- `loading?: boolean`{.shiki,shiki-themes,material-theme-lighter,material-theme,material-theme-palenight lang="ts-type"}
18842	- `disabled?: boolean`{.shiki,shiki-themes,material-theme-lighter,material-theme,material-theme-palenight lang="ts-type"}
18843	- [`slot?: string`{.shiki,shiki-themes,material-theme-lighter,material-theme,material-theme-palenight lang="ts-type"}](https://ui.nuxt.com/#with-custom-slot)
18844	- `placeholder?: string`{.shiki,shiki-themes,material-theme-lighter,material-theme,material-theme-palenight lang="ts-type"}
18845	- `children?: CommandPaletteItem[]`{.shiki,shiki-themes,material-theme-lighter,material-theme,material-theme-palenight lang="ts-type"}
18846	- `onSelect?: (e: Event) => void`{.shiki,shiki-themes,material-theme-lighter,material-theme,material-theme-palenight lang="ts-type"}
18847	- `class?: any`{.shiki,shiki-themes,material-theme-lighter,material-theme,material-theme-palenight lang="ts-type"}
18848	- `ui?: { item?: ClassNameValue, itemLeadingIcon?: ClassNameValue, itemLeadingAvatarSize?: ClassNameValue, itemLeadingAvatar?: ClassNameValue, itemLeadingChipSize?: ClassNameValue, itemLeadingChip?: ClassNameValue, itemLabel?: ClassNameValue, itemLabelPrefix?: ClassNameValue, itemLabelBase?: ClassNameValue, itemLabelSuffix?: ClassNameValue, itemTrailing?: ClassNameValue, itemTrailingKbds?: ClassNameValue, itemTrailingKbdsSize?: ClassNameValue, itemTrailingHighlightedIcon?: ClassNameValue, itemTrailingIcon?: ClassNameValue }`{.shiki,shiki-themes,material-theme-lighter,material-theme,material-theme-palenight lang="ts-type"}
18849	
18850	You can pass any property from the [Link](https://ui.nuxt.com/docs/components/link#props) component such as `to`, `target`, etc.
18851	
18852	```vue
18853	<script setup lang="ts">
18854	import type { CommandPaletteGroup } from '@nuxt/ui'
18855	</script>
18856	
18857	<template>
18858	  <UCommandPalette class="flex-1" />
18859	</template>
18860	```
18861	
18862	\> \[!TIP]
18863	\> See: #with-children-in-items
18864	\> Each item can take a \`children\` array of objects with the following properties to create submenus:
18865	
18866	### Multiple
18867	
18868	Use the `multiple` prop to allow multiple selections.
18869	
18870	```vue
18871	<script setup lang="ts">
18872	import type { CommandPaletteGroup } from '@nuxt/ui'
18873	</script>
18874	
18875	<template>
18876	  <UCommandPalette multiple class="flex-1" />
18877	</template>
18878	```
18879	
18880	\> \[!CAUTION]
18881	\> Ensure to pass an array to the \`default-value\` prop or the \`v-model\` directive.
18882	
18883	### Placeholder
18884	
18885	Use the `placeholder` prop to change the placeholder text.
18886	
18887	```vue
18888	<script setup lang="ts">
18889	import type { CommandPaletteGroup } from '@nuxt/ui'
18890	</script>
18891	
18892	<template>
18893	  <UCommandPalette placeholder="Search an app..." class="flex-1" />
18894	</template>
18895	```
18896	
18897	### Size `4.4+`
18898	
18899	Use the `size` prop to change the size of the CommandPalette.
18900	
18901	```vue
18902	<script setup lang="ts">
18903	import type { CommandPaletteGroup } from '@nuxt/ui'
18904	</script>
18905	
18906	<template>
18907	  <UCommandPalette size="xl" class="flex-1" />
18908	</template>
18909	```
18910	
18911	### Icon
18912	
18913	Use the `icon` prop to customize the input [Icon](https://ui.nuxt.com/docs/components/icon). Defaults to `i-lucide-search`.
18914	
18915	```vue
18916	<script setup lang="ts">
18917	import type { CommandPaletteGroup } from '@nuxt/ui'
18918	</script>
18919	
18920	<template>
18921	  <UCommandPalette icon="i-lucide-box" class="flex-1" />
18922	</template>
18923	```
18924	
18925	\*\*Nuxt:\*\*
18926	\> \[!TIP]
18927	\> See: /docs/getting-started/integrations/icons/nuxt#theme
18928	\> You can customize this icon globally in your \`app.config.ts\` under \`ui.icons.search\` key.
18929	\*\*Vue:\*\*
18930	\> \[!TIP]
18931	\> See: /docs/getting-started/integrations/icons/vue#theme
18932	\> You can customize this icon globally in your \`vite.config.ts\` under \`ui.icons.search\` key.
18933	
18934	### Selected Icon
18935	
18936	Use the `selected-icon` prop to customize the selected item [Icon](https://ui.nuxt.com/docs/components/icon). Defaults to `i-lucide-check`.
18937	
18938	```vue
18939	<script setup lang="ts">
18940	import type { CommandPaletteGroup } from '@nuxt/ui'
18941	</script>
18942	
18943	<template>
18944	  <UCommandPalette multiple selected-icon="i-lucide-circle-check" class="flex-1" />
18945	</template>

raw.content:

18903	import type { CommandPaletteGroup } from '@nuxt/ui'
18904	</script>
18905	
18906	<template>
18907	  <UCommandPalette size="xl" class="flex-1" />
18908	</template>
18909	```
18910	
18911	### Icon
18912	
18913	Use the `icon` prop to customize the input [Icon](https://ui.nuxt.com/docs/components/icon). Defaults to `i-lucide-search`.
18914	
18915	```vue
18916	<script setup lang="ts">
18917	import type { CommandPaletteGroup } from '@nuxt/ui'
18918	</script>
18919	
18920	<template>
18921	  <UCommandPalette icon="i-lucide-box" class="flex-1" />
18922	</template>
18923	```
18924	
18925	\*\*Nuxt:\*\*
18926	\> \[!TIP]
18927	\> See: /docs/getting-started/integrations/icons/nuxt#theme
18928	\> You can customize this icon globally in your \`app.config.ts\` under \`ui.icons.search\` key.
18929	\*\*Vue:\*\*
18930	\> \[!TIP]
18931	\> See: /docs/getting-started/integrations/icons/vue#theme
18932	\> You can customize this icon globally in your \`vite.config.ts\` under \`ui.icons.search\` key.
18933	
18934	### Selected Icon
18935	
18936	Use the `selected-icon` prop to customize the selected item [Icon](https://ui.nuxt.com/docs/components/icon). Defaults to `i-lucide-check`.
18937	
18938	```vue
18939	<script setup lang="ts">
18940	import type { CommandPaletteGroup } from '@nuxt/ui'
18941	</script>
18942	
18943	<template>
18944	  <UCommandPalette multiple selected-icon="i-lucide-circle-check" class="flex-1" />
18945	</template>
